admin_org.description: Requests the GitHub admin:org scope so Auth0 can fully manage organizations, teams, and memberships on behalf of the user.

admin_public_key.description: Requests the admin:public_key scope to allow creating, updating, and deleting the user's SSH public keys.

admin_repo_hook.description: Requests the admin:repo_hook scope so Auth0 can read, write, ping, and delete repository webhooks.

delete_repo.description: Requests the delete_repo scope so the user can remove repositories they administer while signing in through Auth0.

email.description: Requests the user:email scope so Auth0 pulls addresses from GitHub's /user/emails endpoint and populates the profile.

follow.description: Requests the user:follow scope to allow following or unfollowing GitHub users for the signed-in account.

gist.description: Requests the gist scope so the application can create or update gists on behalf of the user.

notifications.description: Requests the notifications scope to read GitHub inbox notifications; repo also implicitly grants this access.

profile.description: Controls the GitHub read:user call that returns the user's basic profile (name, avatar, profile URL) and is on by default for successful logins.

public_repo.description: Requests the public_repo scope for read and write operations on public repositories, deployments, and statuses.

read_org.description: Requests the read:org scope so Auth0 can view organizations, teams, and membership lists without making changes.

read_public_key.description: Requests the read:public_key scope so Auth0 can list and inspect the user's SSH public keys.

read_repo_hook.description: Requests the read:repo_hook scope to read and ping repository webhooks.

read_user.description: Requests the read:user scope to load extended profile information, implicitly covering user:email and user:follow.

repo.description: Requests the repo scope for read and write access to both public and private repositories, deployments, and statuses.

repo_deployment.description: Requests the repo_deployment scope in order to read and write deployment statuses for repositories.

repo_status.description: Requests the repo:status scope to manage commit statuses on public and private repositories.

write_org.description: Requests the write:org scope so Auth0 can change whether organization memberships are publicized.

write_public_key.description: Requests the write:public_key scope to create or update SSH public keys for the user.

write_repo_hook.description: Requests the write:repo_hook scope so Auth0 can read, create, update, and ping repository webhooks.
